summary: Summary: The conversation began with Olivia contacting Viacom Services and waiting on hold for about 30 minutes, experiencing frustration and anger while Speaker2 (the Viacom representative) remained neutral and verified her identity. Olivia (also referenced as Sara Brown in parts of the call) described phone failures—unable to receive calls on work and home lines—which escalated her frustration and anger as proposed fixes failed. Speaker2 stayed neutral, placed her on hold at one point, explained widespread outages (natural disaster-related), and offered solutions including a three-month free service in exchange for a one-year contract and alternatives like using a workplace phone or payphones. Olivia rejected the contract offer and complicated suggestions with anger and increased frustration, demanded to speak to a supervisor, threatened to report the representative, and ultimately ended the call in frustration and anger. Overall, the dialogue followed an escalation from neutral verification and troubleshooting by Speaker2 to mounting frustration and anger from Olivia/Sara, with the representative maintaining a neutral stance while proposing accommodations that the caller refused.
